items.question: How would you deploy feature selection without train/serve skew?
items.answer: Wrap preprocessing, selector, and model in one sklearn Pipeline, fit on training data only, serialize the fitted pipeline (e.g. joblib), and load that artifact at inference so the same support mask and transforms apply to every request.

items.question: Explain why fitting SelectKBest on all data before a train/test split causes leakage.
items.answer: SelectKBest uses supervised scores (e.g. mutual information with y). Test rows contribute to which features are ranked and kept, so information from the test set influences training inputs and metrics are optimistically biased.

items.question: When would you chain a filter before RFECV in a production pipeline?
items.answer: When column count is very large and wrapper cost is too high—use variance or MI filters to reduce to a few hundred columns, then run RFECV on that pool inside a Pipeline with cross-validation for subset size.

items.question: How does this topic differ from feature selection under dimensionality reduction?
items.answer: The dimensionality-reduction topic covers statistical methods, MI, L1 geometry, and PCA contrast; this topic covers pipeline placement, leakage prevention, serialization, feature-store contracts, and operationalizing selection in production workflows.
